executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 104 confirmed Canadian victims disclosed on June 14th across all economic sectors. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 21 victims including critical infrastructure (Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre), followed by Clop with 13 victims and Play with 11. High-profile targets include Canada Goose, Equine Canada, a Peterborough Public Health unit, the Municipality of North Perth, and National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest gang). This coordinated wave impacts healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and manufacturing—indicating either a mass exploitation campaign or coordinated affiliate activity across 18 distinct ransomware families.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware event on June 14, 2026, with 104 organizations publicly listed as victims across 18 different ransomware families. Critical infrastructure sectors were hit including public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart college), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and workplace safety (Workers Health & Safety Centre). No CCCS advisories were issued during this 48-hour window.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer brand) compromised by CoinbaseCartel; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) hit by Sinobi; Kids & Company (childcare services) also compromised by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi; Trucash financial services compromised by Lynx; multiple accounting firms impacted (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, reidhurstnagy.com)

report_2w.executive_summary: Microsoft patched a record 206 Windows vulnerabilities on June 11, including 3 actively exploited zero-days that require immediate patching. The ShinyHunters threat group claimed responsibility for breaching the University of Nottingham (454,600 students affected) and appears to be conducting a mass Oracle PeopleSoft server attack campaign. GitHub is disabling npm install scripts by default to combat a surge in supply chain attacks, while Interpol successfully dismantled the decade-old SniperDz phishing-as-a-service platform. South Korea fined Coupang a record $409 million for a 2025 data breach, and VRChat disclosed a cloud breach exposing 2.4 million user records.

items.description: Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerability actively exploited as zero-day since May 14
SolarWinds Serv-U DoS vulnerability actively exploited
Critical vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools Updates Engine component
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability exploited by China-linked reconnaissance botnet

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: University of Nottingham (454,600 students), Oracle PeopleSoft servers (mass campaign)
items.ttps: T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1530 - Data from Cloud Storage, T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: SniperDz (PhaaS)
items.targets: Global phishing campaigns targeting brands for spoofing, browser hijacking, and CPA fraud
items.ttps: T1566 - Phishing, T1185 - Browser Session Hijacking, T1583.001 - Acquire Infrastructure: Domains (aged domain acquisition)

items.name: DragonForce (Ransomware)
items.targets: Brian Cox, Hong Kong Parkview
items.ttps: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: Qilin (Ransomware)
items.targets: Maui Divers Jewelry, Check Point VPN authentication bypass incidents
items.ttps: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1078 - Valid Accounts, T1133 - External Remote Services

items.name: Akira (Ransomware)
items.targets: Swiss Federal Office for Cybersecurity (RUAG)
items.ttps: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 - Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: China-linked APT (unnamed)
items.targets: Enterprise networks globally with reconnaissance botnet
items.ttps: T1595 - Active Scanning, T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application (CVE-2026-35616)

items.name: North Korean APT (unnamed)
items.targets: Oracle PeopleSoft servers
items.ttps: T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application

canada_landscape.summary: No specific Canadian cybersecurity incidents, breaches, or CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period. Articles mentioning Canada focused on non-cybersecurity topics including trade policy, social issues, and infrastructure. One Mastodon post referenced Saab's surveillance jet deal with Canada facing U.S. stealth technology hurdles, though this relates to procurement rather than active threats.
